typecheck.output: Success: no issues found in 55 source files

lint.command: cd /Users/stefano/repos/open-marcus/backend-python && source venv/bin/activate && ruff check src/

blockingIssues.description: change_password() in password_lock.py does NOT re-encrypt the database with the new key. It only updates the config with new salt/password hash, but the .db.enc file remains encrypted with the OLD key. This allows the old password to still decrypt the database after a password change. Fix: decrypt with old key, then re-encrypt with new key.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Specify exact cryptographic choices in Security Model section: PBKDF2-SHA256 with 480k iterations for key derivation, Fernet (AES-128-CBC + HMAC-SHA256) for symmetric encryption. Also document that change_password requires re-encryption of the database.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: privacy-encryption review found AGENTS.md only says 'Master password -> argon2 -> database encryption key' but implementation uses PBKDF2 and Fernet. Also, change_password re-encryption requirement is not documented.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md Off-Limits section doesn't list analytics prohibition explicitly as convention
rejectedObservations.reason: Ambiguous - the privacy-no-analytics review found AGENTS.md does list 'Do not add any analytics or telemetry' under Off-Limits, so no gap exists
